title: Cabinet paper – Census transformation progress update: Realising the potential of government data | Stats NZ
content:
## Cabinet paper – Census transformation progress update: Realising the potential of government data
23 July 2017, 10:45am
This 2016 paper provides a progress update on transforming the New Zealand census to a model primarily based on administrative data.
It highlights progress on improving the quality of key administrative data across government and identifies what is required to further progress this work.
In addition, the paper provides an update on developing required statistical modelling, exploring new data sources, confirming key customer needs, and further understanding the risks and costs of census transformation.
Census transformation progress update: Releasing the potential of government data was signed by the Minister of Statistics on 18 October 2016, considered by the Cabinet Economic Growth and Infrastructure Committee on 2 November 2016, and confirmed by Cabinet on 7 November 2016.
- Census transformation progress update: Realising the potential of government data – Cabinet paper (redacted) Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 383 KB
